review_focus: Combined backend, frontend, and migration review for PR #4

findings.title: Canonical-name migration skips user-owned tables that now depend on exact matches
findings.description: The canonical-name backfill only covers foodAssessments, ingredientExposures, foodTrialSummary, and foodAliases. foodLibrary, ingredientProfiles, and ingredientOverrides also store canonicalName and now read/write through resolveCanonicalFoodName, so renamed canonicals from this PR will stay stale and later writes will create duplicates or miss existing rows.
findings.fix: Add those tables to the canonicalization migration or run a dedicated backfill before deploying the new registry.

findings.title: Weekly suggestion queries no longer see legacy reportSuggestions data
findings.description: suggestionsByDateRange() only flattens aiAnalyses.insight.suggestions. Existing rows that still live in the deleted reportSuggestions table are invisible, so older reports lose suggestion history until every analysis is re-extracted or migrated.
findings.fix: Union the legacy table during the transition, or add a migration that copies old suggestions into aiAnalyses.insight.suggestions before removing the table.

findings.title: Backup restore drops knownFoods and encryptedApiKey from profiles
findings.description: The profile import block only reinstates the pre-existing profile fields. Exported profile rows now contain knownFoods and encryptedApiKey, but importBackup ignores them, so restoring a backup loses the weekly-digest cache and the server-stored API key.
findings.fix: Carry the new profile fields through export/import, or explicitly backfill them after restore if that omission is intentional.

findings.title: reportSuggestions is no longer round-trippable in backups
findings.description: reportSuggestions was removed from USER_DATA_TABLES, and the import path now silently skips the field. Any backup created before the table removal restores without its suggestion rows, so historical suggestion context is lost.
findings.fix: Keep exporting/importing the legacy table until the data is migrated, or move old rows into aiAnalyses.insight.suggestions before dropping it.

findings.title: Known-foods backfill never creates a profile for legacy users
findings.description: backfillKnownFoodsWorker returns done immediately when the profile row is missing, and addToKnownFoods() is also a no-op without a profile. Users who have logs but never created a profile will never get knownFoods backfilled, so weekly digest keeps treating their foods as new.
findings.fix: Create a minimal profile during backfill, or store knownFoods somewhere that exists before profile creation.

findings.title: Known-foods backfill skips logs that share the last timestamp in a batch
findings.description: The worker resumes with cursor: lastTimestamp + 1 while querying gte(timestamp, cursor). Any logs written in the same millisecond as the final item in a full batch will be skipped permanently on the next page.
findings.fix: Resume with the exact last timestamp and a stable tie-breaker, or paginate by document id instead of timestamp alone.

findings.title: API keys are only base64-obfuscated before storing them in Convex
findings.description: obfuscateApiKey()/deobfuscateApiKey() use btoa/atob, so the server-side key is reversible in the database, backups, and any admin read access. That does not provide at-rest protection for a secret.
findings.fix: Use real at-rest encryption or an external secret store; avoid exporting the key in backups.

findings.title: Read-then-insert profile upsert can create duplicate rows and strand an API key
findings.description: storeApiKey() does a .first() lookup and then inserts when no row exists. Because profiles has only a non-unique by_userId index, concurrent saves or migration+save races can insert two rows. getApiKey()/deleteApiKey() also use .first(), so later reads/deletes can hit the wrong row and leave a stale key behind.
findings.fix: Make profile creation atomic or self-healing: reuse the existing patchProfile/replaceProfile path, or add a duplicate cleanup pass/uniqueness guarantee before inserting.

findings.title: ingredientProfiles upsert can duplicate rows under concurrent writes
findings.description: This mutation has the same read-then-insert pattern on by_userId_canonicalName that ingredientOverrides.ts had to self-heal. Without deduplication or a uniqueness guarantee, two concurrent saves can create duplicate ingredientProfiles for the same user/canonicalName, and list()/byIngredient() will then return nondeterministic results.
findings.fix: Adopt the same duplicate-merge/self-heal pattern used in ingredientOverrides.ts, or centralize the write behind a single atomic profile upsert.

findings.title: hasApiKey ignores the server-stored key and blocks fallback matching
findings.description: The hook only reports apiKey !== null from IndexedDB. useFoodLlmMatching returns early when hasApiKey is false, so a user who loses local storage but still has the Convex-stored key can no longer trigger AI matching even though the server action can fall back to the profile key.
findings.fix: Treat the server key as a valid signal too, or allow the hook to call the action with an empty key when the server copy exists.

findings.title: Failed-save rollback drops the selected date
findings.description: The optimistic clear saves timeValue and activePreset, but the catch block only restores those two fields. dateValue is never restored, so a rejected backdated food log retries with today's date instead of the chosen day.
findings.fix: Snapshot dateValue alongside the other form state and restore it in the failure path.

findings.title: Date-only entries display the wrong timestamp
findings.description: displayTime falls back to the current time, while an empty time field is resolved to midnight by usePanelTime. The trigger therefore says 'Now' or the current clock time even though the saved timestamp will be 00:00.
findings.fix: Show the resolved saved time instead of the current time when the field is empty, or initialize the input from the selected date on mount.

findings.title: Transit network is rebuilt twice per render
findings.description: TransitMapWithLabels already passes a memoized network prop, but the canvas still calls useTransitMapData(foodStats) unconditionally, duplicating the expensive registry/evidence merge and discarding the parent result whenever networkProp is provided.
findings.fix: Only call useTransitMapData when networkProp is absent, or hoist the hook call so the parent is the single source of truth.

findings.title: Station-row rendering should be memoized
findings.description: Changing selectedCanonical rerenders the full line.stations.map tree for every station row. With roughly 147 stations in the transit map, memoizing the row component would avoid most of the churn when selecting a single station.
findings.fix: Wrap the station row component in React.memo or split out the selected/highlighted subrow so only the affected station rerenders.
